title: Exposing administration services in containers is security-sensitive
description: Exposing administration services in containers is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotdockerfilecweExposing administration services can lead to unauthorized access to containers or escalation of privilege inside of containers.A port that is commonly used for administration services is marked as being open through theEXPOSEcommand. Administration services like SSH might contain vulnerabilities, hard-coded credentials, or other security issues that increase the attack surface of a Docker deployment.Even if the ports of the services do not get forwarded to the host system, by default they are reachable from other containers in the same network. A malicious actor that gets access to one container could use such services to escalate access and privi
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Exposing administration services in containers is security-sensitive. Port commonly used for administration services (SSH:22, RDP:3389, Telnet:23, VNC:5900-5903, Database:3306/5432/27017, etc.) should not be exposed. These services may contain vulnerabilities or provide unauthorized access. Consider using secure alternatives or restricting access through network policies.
